task_completion.description: GIMP is opened with the initial canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible, displaying a blank white canvas.

task_completion.description: The Filters menu or action search is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Filters' menu in the top menu bar is expanded, or the action search dialog (accessed via '/') is open.

task_completion.description: The Solid Noise filter is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Solid Noise' option is highlighted in the menu (under Render -> Clouds) or in the search results.

task_completion.description: The Solid Noise dialog box is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Solid Noise' dialog window is visible on screen, showing a preview and parameters like X size, Y size, and Detail.

task_completion.description: The OK button in the Solid Noise dialog is clicked to apply the filter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The mouse cursor is clicking the 'OK' button in the Solid Noise dialog, or the dialog is closed in the subsequent step.

task_completion.description: The procedural noise texture is successfully generated on the canvas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main GIMP canvas is filled with a grayscale, cloud-like procedural noise pattern, replacing the blank white canvas.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to apply the filter.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP's graphical interface rather than terminal commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The Solid Noise filter was actually used.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Solid Noise dialog or menu path is visible in the screenshots, confirming the filter was used rather than importing an external noise image.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripting consoles to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: No Python-Fu or Script-Fu console windows are open or used to execute the filter.

privileged_info_for_vlm: GIMP 2.10 is used. The Solid Noise filter is located at Filters -> Render -> Clouds -> Solid Noise. The filter generates a grayscale procedural noise texture (cloud-like pattern) that completely overwrites the existing layer. The filter dialog includes parameters like X size, Y size, Detail, Tileable, and Turbulent.
